description: Enterprise security documentation corpus for SWEObeyMe
purpose: Provide comprehensive enterprise security documentation for SSO, RBAC, SCIM, compliance, and multi-tenant features

documents.title: The Little Book of OAuth 2.0 RFCs
documents.author: Aaron Parecki
documents.description: Comprehensive guide to OAuth 2.0 RFCs and specifications
documents.relevance: Understanding OAuth 2.0 protocol for SSO implementation

documents.title: Role-Based Access Control - Auth0 Docs
documents.author: Auth0
documents.description: Comprehensive RBAC implementation guide from Auth0
documents.relevance: Understanding RBAC patterns for enterprise access control

categories.name: scim
categories.description: SCIM (System for Cross-domain Identity Management) protocol documentation

documents.title: RFC 7644 - SCIM Protocol
documents.author: IETF
documents.relevance: Understanding SCIM protocol for identity provisioning

documents.title: GDPR Compliance Checklist
documents.author: LW
documents.description: Comprehensive GDPR compliance checklist and requirements
documents.relevance: Understanding GDPR compliance requirements for enterprise features

instructions.addingDocuments: Place resources in appropriate subdirectories. Update this index.json with document metadata including title, author, description, license, topics, and relevance.
instructions.downloadingSources: Download from IETF RFCs, official OAuth documentation, compliance standard bodies, and reputable security resources. Prioritize official specifications and standards.
instructions.namingConvention: Use clear, descriptive filenames with underscores: spec_name.pdf, guide_name.html
instructions.licenseCompliance: Include license information for all resources. Prioritize official IETF, OAuth, and compliance standard documentation.
instructions.securityFocus: Resources should enable SWEObeyMe to: (1) understand SSO/OAuth implementation patterns, (2) implement RBAC correctly, (3) support SCIM provisioning, (4) meet compliance standards (SOC2/GDPR/HIPAA), (5) implement encryption properly, (6) handle multi-tenant isolation.
